introSection: Banshee is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Kings Island in Mason, Ohio, USA. It is the first inverted roller coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard in the United States since Patriot at Worlds of Fun opened in 2006.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In 1995, Cedar Point originally intended to use the name "Banshee" on Mantis, the Bolliger & Mabillard stand up roller coaster, but the name was changed once the park realized the negative connotations of the word.

Banshee is partially located on the site of the former Son of Beast wooden roller coaster and the Thunder Alley go-kart track. After remaining standing but not operating for three years, Son of Beast was demolished in 2012.

Banshee was announced on August 8, 2013 at 10 PM, Ohio time. The first pieces arrived at the park on August 16, 2013. Construction began that month with the installation of the first track piece. In September 2013, the lift hill was topped off. The ride was completed in January 2014. Testing of the ride began on March 15, 2014.

On April 18, 2014, Banshee opened to the public.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The lift hill threads the vertical loop, just like Riddler's Revenge at Six Flags Magic Mountain and Kumba at Busch Gardens Tampa. Banshee ties with Montu at Busch Gardens Tampa for most inversions on an inverted coaster, with seven.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: As the train leaves the station, it makes a left turn and climbs the 167 foot lift hill. At the top, the banshee scream is heard and the train descends 150 feet, reaching. The train flips into a tall dive loop, followed by a  tall vertical loop. The train then goes into a tall zero-g roll. Following that is an tall pretzel knot. The train goes into a second vertical loop at, spiral right and goes into an in-line twist at. After the helix, the train hits the final brake run and returns into the station.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Banshee has a raspberry track color and blue supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: This is the first B&M invert to utilize vest harnesses, instead of the traditional over-the-shoulder harnesses that all other B&M inverts have.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: A 38-year-old man was struck and killed by the roller coaster in June 2024 after entering a restricted area.

fullText:
Banshee is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Kings Island in Mason, Ohio, USA. It is the first inverted roller coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard in the United States since Patriot at Worlds of Fun opened in 2006.

In 1995, Cedar Point originally intended to use the name "Banshee" on Mantis, the Bolliger & Mabillard stand up roller coaster, but the name was changed once the park realized the negative connotations of the word.

Banshee is partially located on the site of the former Son of Beast wooden roller coaster and the Thunder Alley go-kart track. After remaining standing but not operating for three years, Son of Beast was demolished in 2012.

Banshee was announced on August 8, 2013 at 10 PM, Ohio time. The first pieces arrived at the park on August 16, 2013. Construction began that month with the installation of the first track piece. In September 2013, the lift hill was topped off. The ride was completed in January 2014. Testing of the ride began on March 15, 2014.

On April 18, 2014, Banshee opened to the public.

The lift hill threads the vertical loop, just like Riddler's Revenge at Six Flags Magic Mountain and Kumba at Busch Gardens Tampa. Banshee ties with Montu at Busch Gardens Tampa for most inversions on an inverted coaster, with seven.

As the train leaves the station, it makes a left turn and climbs the 167 foot lift hill. At the top, the banshee scream is heard and the train descends 150 feet, reaching. The train flips into a tall dive loop, followed by a  tall vertical loop. The train then goes into a tall zero-g roll. Following that is an tall pretzel knot. The train goes into a second vertical loop at, spiral right and goes into an in-line twist at. After the helix, the train hits the final brake run and returns into the station.

Banshee has a raspberry track color and blue supports.

This is the first B&M invert to utilize vest harnesses, instead of the traditional over-the-shoulder harnesses that all other B&M inverts have.

A 38-year-old man was struck and killed by the roller coaster in June 2024 after entering a restricted area.
